sources_audited.note: The Census archive defines 312112 as establishments primarily purifying and bottling water, including naturally carbonated water, and gives the stated cross-references. It is a 1997 classification archive rather than a current NAICS page.

The May 2023 BLS NAICS 312100 table reports the cited employment shares: production 21.59%, installation/maintenance 5.29%, management 5.33%, office/administrative 4.68%, business/financial 3.48%, and food preparation/serving 26.40%.
The Census article reports BTOS data collected December 14, 2025 through May 3, 2026, with current AI use at 17%-20%, expected six-month use at 20%-23%, and higher reported use among larger firms.

NIST lists predictive maintenance, quality control, demand forecasting, document management, inventory-related deployment, supply-chain optimization, and production scheduling, along with data, cost, skills, cybersecurity, and legacy-integration barriers.

FDA states that bottled-water CGMPs require sanitary processing, source protection, quality control, source- and final-product sampling and testing, and describes FDA inspection of plants and operations.

The FDA guide accurately summarizes the underlying rule's weekly source testing for non-public-water sources, follow-up E. coli testing, corrective measures and records, and finished-product follow-up testing. The guidance itself is nonbinding, although it describes regulatory requirements.

Beverage Marketing reports 2.9% US bottled-water volume growth in 2024 after 0.4% in 2023 and 1.1% in 2022, 47.3 gallons per capita in 2024, and an expectation of reaching 50 gallons around decade-end.

The SEC-filed agreement supports the five-year initial term, exclusive manufacturing arrangement, minimum 3.2 million annual cases, $2-per-case shortfall mechanism, 60% soft-drink-PPI and 20% PET-resin adjustments, and 3% raw-material loss allowance.

The Primo filing describes independent bottlers producing purified water to customer specifications and quality requirements, customer-provided bottles, caps, labels and other inputs, generally three-year terms, and customer pricing modification on thirty days' notice.

The 2025 Bottled Water Reporter PDF gives total US volume of 15,702.6, 15,880.9, 15,947.3, and 16,404.4 million gallons for 2021-2024 and a 70.7% single-serve PET share in 2024, matching the packet's rounded figures.

caveats: The sharply narrowed co-packer/private-label lens is disclosed and coherent, but neither the cited NAICS definition nor the contract examples measure the eligible share of current LMM firms; e and s5 remain consequential bounded estimates.

The a and rho values translate four-digit beverage employment and broad business/manufacturing AI evidence into bottled-water plant task exposure and five-year implementation without plant-level wage-weighted task or deployment data.

The q proxy rests on two old, nonrepresentative arrangements: a large-scale Niagara co-packing contract and Primo's 2010 exchange-network bottler program. They establish possible contract mechanisms, not a current LMM retention distribution.

The d5 range uses total bottled-water demand across imports, vended water, home-and-office delivery, retail bulk, sparkling, and integrated branded output; it does not directly establish outsourced LMM co-packer volume, so its near-flat downside is judgmental.

S1 is a 1997 NAICS archive and S6 is nonbinding guidance summarizing binding rules. Both support the uses made here, but their vintage or document character should not be mistaken for current firm-population or contract evidence.
summary: Mechanics pass and all ten primitive-cited sources are reachable and support the stated source facts. The record is useful and candid about its major evidence gaps, with estimates governing eligible-firm share, transfer probability, and operator necessity and broad proxies governing task exposure, implementation, retention, and demand. The disclosed weaknesses and source representativeness issues do not constitute material defects under the validator test because correcting or downgrading them on the evidence reviewed would not change the STRUCTURAL_OUT base tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.
